title: Diagnostics, Build Reports, Metrics, Profiles, and Failure Triage · Program Brief: Build Metrics
summary: The command prints unordered warnings without locations, hides skipped pages, reports only total duration, logs author content and local paths, and cannot compare a slow build with a prior release. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the build metrics increment in bounded actionable build and publication evidence report. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.
Sort deterministically, deduplicate, cap cascades, promote configured warnings, summarize omissions, and return status from policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-build-metrics — Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-diagnostics-observability-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-diagnostics-observability-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_diagnostics_observability_4(diagnostics: int = 7, located: int = 7, build_stages: int = 10, measured_stages: int = 10, unredacted_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if diagnostics < 0 or located < 0:
        return False, located, "diagnostic-count"
    if located != diagnostics:
        return False, located, "location-coverage"
    if build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages:
        return False, measured_stages, "metric-coverage"
    if unredacted_secrets > 0:
        return False, unredacted_secrets, "diagnostic-leak"
    assert located == diagnostics
    return True, measured_stages, "actionable-build-report"

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest. Artifact under review: bounded actionable build and publication evidence report.

options.text: For inspect build metrics evidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Build Metrics for bounded actionable build and publication evidence report.
The record names the assumption, applies Build Metrics, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Build Metrics record says bounded actionable build and publication evidence report looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect build metrics evidence boundary for Build Metrics.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Build Metrics model · Theory Build Metrics
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Build Metrics: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 7846 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Build Metrics model · Theory Build Metrics.
steps.why: Retrieving Build Metrics through the concrete bounded actionable build and publication evidence report case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Build Metrics model · Theory Build Metrics, keep Build Metrics connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Build Metrics model · Theory Build Metrics, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Build Metrics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Build Metrics model · Theory Build Metrics, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7846 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Build Metrics model · Theory Build Metrics, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Total build time is enough observability for a generator.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-build-metrics-jrkv17(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_build_metrics_jrkv17\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?diagnostics < 0 or located < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?located != diagnostics)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unredacted_secrets > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
